_schema_note: jpods-map-v2 DRAFT — 2026-05-20. Full physical network document. Written by Noelle on every Build/Validate. One source of truth for all consumers: SketchUp visualizer, TripPlanner, Nora (Pi), Alice (billing), Route-Time, Dispatcher. All distances in mm. All coordinates in mm (x=east, y=north, z=up from model origin). Converges SketchUp and podPresenter formats. Allie and Noelle: annotate _innovation fields with observations and candidates for next iteration.
_innovation_global: ALLIE: what cross-domain data belongs at the map level vs. line level? (e.g. solar capacity of the whole network, CO2 offset, cargo throughput ceiling)
NOELLE: should mapId carry a hash of the followme.json it was derived from, so Nora can detect stale maps?
NOELLE: add a 'last_physical_survey' timestamp — date/time Nora last traversed every line and confirmed physical.json is current
ALLIE: Route-Time needs O-D travel times. Should map.json carry nominal_time_s per line at nominal speed, so Route-Time never recalculates fixed geometry?

lines._note: Every guideway segment — intra-station (gw_*) and inter-station (seg_*) — is a complete self-contained record. Consumers never re-derive geometry from SketchUp entities. Key: same id used in trip.json segments.

S048.gw_platform._note: Intra-station line. Role = gw_platform means this is the loading/unloading track. Pods dwell here for passenger exchange.

segments._note: grade_pct = dz/len * 100. Zero here — flat platform. Uphill positive, downhill negative. Nora adjusts motor power by grade.

S048.gw_platform._note_markers: AprilTag markers on this line. Each marker: {id, dist_mm, x_mm, y_mm, z_mm, value}. dist_mm = distance from startPoint where marker is mounted on guideway structure. Physical Nora reads AprilTag value to confirm position.
S048.gw_platform._note_platform_capacity: INNOVATION CANDIDATE: how many pods can occupy gw_platform simultaneously without collision. Derived from length_mm / nominal_pod_length_mm. Noelle can calculate; Dispatcher reads.
S048.gw_platform._note_dwell_time: INNOVATION CANDIDATE: nominal passenger exchange time. Alice uses for schedule. Nora uses for departure timer. Could be template-declared in noelle_features.json and overridden per instance.
S048.gw_platform._note_cargo_direction: INNOVATION CANDIDATE: inbound | outbound | bidirectional | none. Platform handles both arriving cargo and departing cargo when bidirectional. Alice routes cargo by direction.

seg_S048_cp1_S050_cp0._note: Inter-station line. Bezier path with waypoints — multiple segments. grade_pct per segment captures vertical profile for Nora motor control and Route-Time energy model.

segments._note: Segments subdivide the Bezier path at ~2m intervals. Populated by Noelle from followme.json track geometry.

seg_S048_cp1_S050_cp0._note_segments: Full segment array omitted from draft — Noelle generates from followme.json path data at Build time. Each 2m step becomes one segment record.

markers._note: Waypoint marker 1 on this connection. Physical: AprilTag mounted on guideway column. dist_mm from startPoint.
markers._note_xy: x/y/z in mm from model origin — Noelle populates from marker entity position at Build time.

energy_model._note: INNOVATION CANDIDATE: per-line energy estimate. Route-Time and Alice use this. Noelle calculates from grade profile + length. Physical Nora logs actual vs. estimated for calibration.

ezones._note: Merge/diverge points where collision risk exists. Nora must check ezone clearance before entering. Same structure as podPresenter — converged.
ezones._note_approach_decel_mm: INNOVATION CANDIDATE: distance before ezone entry where Nora begins deceleration ramp. Derived from ezMaxSpeed and normal speed. Noelle can calculate; Nora reads.

dead_end_nodes._note: Lines that terminate without a successor — line-end stations. Nora must stop before these. Noelle flags them.

network_summary._note: INNOVATION CANDIDATE: top-level network stats. Alice uses for capacity planning. Route-Time uses for network-wide metrics. Noelle calculates at Build.
network_summary._note_solar: Noelle counts solar panel component instances at Build. Alice uses for energy offset calculation.
